products.name: Cocoa & Coffee
products.summary: Quality cocoa sourced from Liberian farms and farming communities.
products.details: ETTA’s cocoa operations are built around direct farmer relationships, careful sourcing, and quality handling. From cocoa pods in the field to aggregated market-ready supply, the company supports a more reliable and transparent cocoa value chain.

products.name: Coffee
products.summary: Responsible coffee sourcing rooted in local agricultural potential.
products.details: ETTA recognizes coffee as part of Liberia’s agricultural opportunity. The company’s coffee direction focuses on farmer engagement, responsible sourcing, and market development for quality supply.

products.name: Agricultural Commodities
products.summary: Broader agricultural trading opportunities built around local production.
products.details: Beyond cocoa and coffee, ETTA is positioned to support agricultural commodity trading where quality, farmer relationships, and market readiness align.

leadership.title: Leadership Rooted in Strategy and Sustainability
leadership.intro: ETTA’s leadership combines agricultural vision, financial discipline, operational structure, and commitment to Liberia’s cocoa and coffee future.

members.name: Ernest M. Jacobs
members.title: Chief Executive Officer
members.bio: Dynamic and results-driven executive with extensive experience in financial management, business administration, strategic planning, and organizational leadership. Possessing a Master of Business Administration (MBA) in Accounting and over five years of progressive leadership experience across finance, administration, auditing, and operational management. Passionate about driving growth and innovation within the cocoa and coffee sector through sustainable business practices, value-chain development, export readiness, financial accountability, and strategic partnerships.

about.title: Rooted in Liberia’s Cocoa and Coffee Future
about.subtitle: ETTA is building a stronger agricultural value chain through farmer partnerships, responsible sourcing, and market-focused operations.
story:
ETTA Trading Corporation ’s story is deeply connected to the origin and legacy of liberica coffee, a bean born in the forests of Liberia and now rediscovered through the dreams of a mother and son.
Liberica coffee grows wild in the tropical forests of Liberia and was first documented by European botanists in the mid-19th century. In the 1890s, when a devastating disease known as coffee leaf rust destroyed major arabica plantations across southeast Asia and India, the world turned to Liberia’s hardy liberica bean as a solution. Its natural resistance to disease, drought, and warmer climates helped save the global coffee trade.
Known for its large, distorted beans and bold, full-bodied flavor with nutty, smoky, floral, and fruity notes, liberica became deeply woven into the culture of southeast Asia, even though Liberia remains its true birthplace. Today, as climate change threatens traditional coffee production, liberica is once again gaining global attention for its resilience and sustainability.
In the rural heart of Nimba county, near the beautiful Mount Nimba where nature and culture unite, lived a hardworking single mother named Benetta and her young son Ernest. They survived through a small acre of liberica coffee inherited from Benetta’s father a farm that became the family’s only source of income and the means to pay Ernest’s school fees. Despite the struggles, Ernest always encouraged his mother, saying, “I believe these trees will one day change our future.”
That belief became the foundation of ETTA Trading Corporation. The name “ETTA” was inspired by both mother and son, symbolizing their journey, sacrifice, resilience, and shared dream for a better future. Today, every package of ETTA coffee carries their story a reminder that every coffee bean represents love, hope, perseverance, and the belief that even the smallest seed can grow into something extraordinary.
Determined to continue his mother’s legacy, Ernest later moved to Monrovia for higher education. Through perseverance and hard work, he earned both his bachelor’s and master’s degrees in business administration from Cottington university. Inspired by his family’s journey and Liberia’s rich coffee heritage, he founded ETTA Trading Corporation transforming a childhood dream rooted in a small family coffee farm into a company committed to reviving liberia’s indigenous liberica coffee and sharing its story with the world.
Today, ETTA Trading Corporation stands not only as a business, but as a bridge between Liberia’s agricultural heritage and the future of sustainable coffee production. Through every harvest, ETTA honors the legacy of liberica coffee a bean that once helped save the global coffee industry and now symbolizes the resilience, pride, and potential of Liberia itself. For European partners and consumers seeking traceable, organic, and impact-driven coffee,  Liberica Coffee grow around the Nimba Mountain offers not only exceptional quality but also a meaningful story of people, place, and purpose.

ETTA Trading Corporation is a Liberian agricultural trading company focused on cocoa, coffee, and responsible commodity sourcing. The company works with farmers and farming communities to strengthen market access, improve product quality, and support sustainable agricultural growth. 

Through direct farmer engagement and market-oriented operations, ETTA is positioned to support Liberia’s role in regional and international agricultural trade while maintaining a strong commitment to trust, transparency, and quality.
about.mission: To empower farmers and farming communities through responsible sourcing, quality-driven agricultural trade, and sustainable access to reliable markets.
about.vision: To become a trusted Liberian agricultural trading company recognized for quality cocoa, coffee, ethical partnerships, and export-ready operations.
